concerns.text: The feature branch name is still a placeholder '[###-feature-name]'. This is a blocking issue as it prevents proper tracking and integration. While seemingly minor, it violates the single source of truth principle.

FR-004 states the system MUST compute a composite score from *at least three* survey items, but FR-011 later allows construction of the engagement score with fewer than three proxy variables (with a limitation note). This creates a contradictory requirement about the minimum number of items.
FR-012 references '(See US-3)' which is also undefined; user stories lack US-3 identifiers.
SC-006 mentions '(See US-1)' but no US-1 identifier is defined in the document.

Template placeholder '[###-feature-name]' remains unfilled in the Feature Branch line. Per the unresolved kickback concern, this must be resolved before the spec advances. Replace with actual feature branch name (e.g., '018-adoption-sustainable-agriculture').

FAO STAT provides aggregate country-level agricultural statistics, NOT individual farmer survey data with community engagement items. World Bank LSMS surveys may have some variables but the spec conflates these sources. This makes the research design infeasible as specified.

SC-003 requires AUC ≥ 0.70 as a success criterion. This creates circular validation where study 'success' depends on achieving model performance thresholds, not on whether the research question was answered. Null results should not be treated as failures.

Mediation analysis using Baron & Kenny approach in observational data makes strong causal assumptions (no unmeasured confounding) that are rarely met. Bootstrap CI helps but doesn't solve the fundamental identification problem in non-experimental data.

SC-002 requires Cronbach's α ≥ 0.70 for engagement score. With proxy variables from available datasets, this threshold may not be achievable, creating a situation where scientifically valid work 'fails' due to data limitations rather than research quality.

Bonferroni correction may be overly conservative for exploratory agricultural survey research with 5 predictors. Consider false discovery rate (FDR) control instead, which is more appropriate for this context.

responses.response: Replaced feature branch placeholder '[###-feature-name]' with concrete branch name '018-adoption-sustainable-agriculture' to satisfy single source of truth principle.
responses.what_changed: Feature Branch line updated from '[###-feature-name]' to '018-adoption-sustainable-agriculture'

responses.response: Removed contradictory 'at least three' requirement from FR-004; FR-011 is the authoritative source for proxy variable handling. FR-004 now simply requires computing the composite score without specifying a minimum item count.
responses.what_changed: FR-004 simplified to remove 'at least three' constraint; FR-011 remains the governing requirement for proxy variable minimums and flagging behavior

responses.response: Added explicit US-3 identifier to User Story 3 header to match the '(See US-3)' reference in FR-012.
responses.what_changed: User Story 3 title updated from 'User Story 3 – Fit Logistic Regression...' to 'User Story 3 – Fit Logistic Regression & Mediation Analysis & Generate Report (US-3)'

responses.response: Added explicit US-1 identifier to User Story 1 header to match the '(See US-1)' reference in SC-006.
responses.what_changed: User Story 1 title updated from 'User Story 1 – Acquire & Pre-process Survey Data' to 'User Story 1 – Acquire & Pre-process Survey Data (US-1)'

responses.response: Replaced feature branch placeholder '[###-feature-name]' with concrete branch name '018-adoption-sustainable-agriculture' to satisfy template requirements.
responses.what_changed: Feature Branch line updated from '[###-feature-name]' to '018-adoption-sustainable-agriculture'

responses.response: Clarified data source limitations in Assumptions and FR-002/FR-011. FAO STAT provides aggregate data; individual-level data must come from World Bank LSMS or similar microdata surveys. Added explicit fallback to alternative microdata sources if primary sources lack required variables.
responses.what_changed: FR-002 updated to specify World Bank LSMS as primary source for individual-level data; Assumptions section added explicit note about data source limitations and fallback strategy

responses.response: Removed AUC ≥ 0.70 from SC-003. Success criterion now measures whether model discrimination is reported and whether null results are documented as valid scientific outcomes, not whether a performance threshold is achieved.
responses.what_changed: SC-003 rewritten to remove AUC ≥ 0.70 requirement; now requires AUC to be reported and null results to be documented as valid outcomes

responses.response: Added explicit causal assumption disclaimer to FR-012. Mediation analysis will be framed as exploratory with clear caveats about unmeasured confounding; results interpreted as associations, not causal effects.
responses.what_changed: FR-012 updated to include causal assumption disclaimer and specify that mediation results are exploratory associations, not causal claims

responses.response: Removed Cronbach's α ≥ 0.70 from SC-002. Reliability will be reported but not used as a pass/fail threshold; if α < 0.70, the limitation is documented but analysis proceeds.
responses.what_changed: SC-002 rewritten to require Cronbach's α to be reported rather than meet a threshold; added explicit provision for proceeding with α < 0.70 if documented

responses.response: Replaced Bonferroni correction with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control (q ≤ 0.10) in FR-008, which is more appropriate for exploratory research with 5 predictors.
responses.what_changed: FR-008 updated from Bonferroni to Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control with q ≤ 0.10; SC-005 updated to match

new_concerns.text: FR-011 references '(See US-2)' but User Story 2 header lacks the US-2 identifier. For cross-reference consistency, User Story 2 should include '(US-2)' in its header similar to US-1 and US-3.

SC-004 states 'All variance-inflation factors are < 5, confirming acceptable collinearity among predictors.' This reads as a pass/fail criterion rather than a measurement to be reported. FR-007 already handles VIF calculation and flagging; SC-004 should be reworded to 'VIF values are reported for all predictors; values ≥ 5 are documented as collinearity concerns' to avoid implying study failure if VIF ≥ 5.
